source_jobs.title: Software Engineer, Product
source_jobs.description:
Midstream is an AI-native financial operating system for health systems, founded by experienced entrepreneurs and backed by top-tier investors. We give finance, supply chain, and managed care teams real-time visibility into where margin is at risk, with a precise action plan for capturing it before it's gone.

Purpose-built for healthcare's complexity, Midstream transforms structured, unstructured, and external data into instant, contract-aware insights that drive smarter decisions. Our AI agents unify spend and revenue operations across the full back-office, learning and adapting with every interaction and delivering always-on intelligence that no point solution or one-off engagement can match.

We're compressing months to minutes and turning post-period hindsight into in-the-moment foresight. The tempo of healthcare finance is changing, and Midstream is leading it.




OPPORTUNITY

This is a rare opportunity to join Midstream at a formative stage and help shape our foundational product. You’ll work at the intersection of product, systems, and cloud infrastructure, designing and building a ML and AI native platform. This role is perfect for mid-senior level engineers who are passionate about user experience and bring a craft that emphasizes scalability, reliability and performance.

This is a hybrid position, based in the Bay Area.




WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING

We’re looking for a driven Software Engineer to take ownership of critical components within our software platform. You’ll work closely with healthcare data, contributing to capabilities that assist medical provider networks, ensuring secure, efficient handling of sensitive information to improve outcomes across the healthcare sector. These responsibilities include some / all of the following:

 - SaaS Platform & Infrastructure: Develop key components of our multi-tenant SaaS platform, including Identity & Access Management, connector provisioning, and infrastructure enhancements to improve speed and service integration.

 - API Development: Play a key role in the design and implementation of our APIs and data models, ensuring they meet the needs of diverse verticals and support both open/shared and customer-specific datasets effectively.

 - ML Platform Development: Leverage existing foundational models and develop novel ML solutions to solve problems with complex, high dimensional healthcare data

 - Agentic workflows: Build tools and and agents to power our natural language analytics that enables rapid discovery of insights with source document traceability.

 - UI Development: Build a world-class user experience into the Midstream platform to provide actionable insights over large data sets.

 - Security & Compliance: Implement and maintain robust security measures, including encryption and role-based access control, while ensuring compliance with standards like SOC 2 and HIPAA

 - Setting standards and delivering impact: You will be a member of the founding engineering team working closely with the founders and have the opportunity to make big early impacts on our systems.

Your work will directly contribute to the platform's evolution, enabling robust data integration and analysis capabilities, supporting machine learning deployment, and ensuring our platform remains secure and compliant.




WHO YOU ARE

 - Problem-Solving: Excellent problem-solving skills with a knack for tackling complex technical challenges.

 - Communication: Strong communication skills, capable of working collaboratively within cross-functional teams and articulating technical concepts to diverse audiences.

 - Adaptability: Ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment, with resilience and effective task prioritization.

 - Programming Skills: Excellent coding abilities in high-level languages such as Python and Typescript

 - SaaS Platform Development: Knowledge in building scalable SaaS platforms, including API design, CI/CD, multi-tenancy, RBAC

 - Cloud Platforms: Strong experience with cl

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer, Systems
source_jobs.description:
Midstream is an AI-native financial operating system for health systems, founded by experienced entrepreneurs and backed by top-tier investors. We give finance, supply chain, and managed care teams real-time visibility into where margin is at risk, with a precise action plan for capturing it before it's gone.

Purpose-built for healthcare's complexity, Midstream transforms structured, unstructured, and external data into instant, contract-aware insights that drive smarter decisions. Our AI agents unify spend and revenue operations across the full back-office, learning and adapting with every interaction and delivering always-on intelligence that no point solution or one-off engagement can match.

We're compressing months to minutes and turning post-period hindsight into in-the-moment foresight. The tempo of healthcare finance is changing, and Midstream is leading it.




THE OPPORTUNITY

This is a rare opportunity to join Midstream at a formative stage and help shape the technical foundation of the platform. You’ll work at the intersection of product, systems, and cloud infrastructure, designing and building the distributed systems that allow a small team to move fast, safely, and with confidence as the company scales.

You’ll partner closely with engineers and leadership to understand where infrastructure creates friction today, and where it will quietly break tomorrow if left unaddressed. From multi-tenant architecture and security boundaries to developer workflows and observability, you’ll turn ambiguity into durable systems that reduce operational overhead and compound engineering velocity.

We’re looking for a systems-minded software engineer who cares deeply about long-term maintainability and developer experience, but who also loves developing backend services and shipping a real software. Someone who can reason about distributed systems, make pragmatic tradeoffs, and build infrastructure that fades into the background, so the team can stay focused on delivering meaningful product value.


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Build shared platform patterns and tooling that help engineers ship new backend services and workflows quickly, safely, and consistently.

 - Strengthen the reliability of our production systems by improving observability, debugging, and resilience as we scale.

 - Design infrastructure that is clean, reviewable, and repeatable—reducing one-off configurations and enabling faster iteration.

 - Establish clear and scalable multi-tenant boundaries across data, compute, and identity to support enterprise healthcare customers.

 - Help ensure the team can spend more time building new capabilities—and less time maintaining systems—as customer usage and data volume grow.

 - Build the agentic orchestration layer for tools and and agents to power our natural language analytics that enables rapid discovery of insights with source document traceability.


WHO YOU ARE

 - You have strong instincts for designing distributed systems and scalable SaaS platforms, with a focus on clear ownership, isolation, and reliability. You understand core platform building blocks like API design, CI/CD, multi-tenancy, and role-based access control, and you build systems that are secure and resilient without unnecessary complexity.

 - You’re deeply comfortable in modern cloud environments (AWS or GCP) and have experience using infrastructure-as-code tools like Terraform and Kubernetes. You know how to leverage cloud-native services for computing, storage, and ML-enabled applications as the platform grows.

 - You treat infrastructure as software and care about developer experience. You enjoy creating reusable patterns, templates, and automation that help teams ship faster with fewer operational burdens.

 - You’re a strong backend engineer who writes high-quality services and pipelines. You’re comfortable building APIs, asynchronous workflows, background jobs, integrations, and foundational systems—balancing infrastructure leadership with del

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer, Frontend
source_jobs.description:
Midstream is an AI-native financial operating system for health systems, founded by experienced entrepreneurs and backed by top-tier investors. We give finance, supply chain, and managed care teams real-time visibility into where margin is at risk, with a precise action plan for capturing it before it's gone.

Purpose-built for healthcare's complexity, Midstream transforms structured, unstructured, and external data into instant, contract-aware insights that drive smarter decisions. Our AI agents unify spend and revenue operations across the full back-office, learning and adapting with every interaction and delivering always-on intelligence that no point solution or one-off engagement can match.

We're compressing months to minutes and turning post-period hindsight into in-the-moment foresight. The tempo of healthcare finance is changing, and Midstream is leading it.




OPPORTUNITY

We are looking for a Software Engineer, Front End to design and build the interaction layer for Midstream’s AI-native product.

This role focuses on how users understand, decide, and act within the product. You will translate complex data and AI-driven responses into clear, intuitive experiences that guide users toward the right decisions rather than just present information.

You’ll work across the stack, with a primary focus on frontend, to build fast, responsive interfaces that make complex workflows and decisions feel simple and reliable. This is an opportunity to help define how AI-native interfaces behave in production systems and over complex datasets, blending chat with structured, guided interactions.




WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING

 - Build the interaction layer for an AI-native product
   
   Design and implement user interfaces that turn complex data, insights, recommendations into clear, actionable workflows.

 - Translate complexity into intuitive experiences
   
   Make complex data systems feel simple, fast, and navigable through strong interaction design and information architecture.

 - Define AI-native UX patterns
   
   Design interfaces that are suggestive, responsive, and trustworthy, guiding users toward decisions rather than leaving them with open-ended outputs.

 - Own frontend systems and architecture
   
   Build scalable frontend components and patterns that enable fast, consistent product development.

 - Work across the stack
   
   Partner closely with backend systems, on APIs, data models, and real-time infrastructure to deliver seamless product experiences.




WHO YOU ARE

 - A strong frontend engineer with product instincts
   
   You build high-quality user experiences and care deeply about usability, interaction design, and performance.

 - Comfortable across the stack
   
   You’re strong in TypeScript, React (or similar), and modern frontend architecture, and comfortable working with APIs and backend systems.

 - Experienced with complex interfaces
   
   You’ve built analytics-heavy or workflow-driven products and know how to make them feel intuitive and fast.

 - Interested in AI-native products
   
   You have experience with or a strong curiosity about agent-driven systems and how they shape user interaction.

 - A builder who thrives in ambiguity
   
   You’re comfortable turning loosely defined problems into intuitive and reliable product experiences.

source_jobs.title: Product Marketing
source_jobs.description:
Midstream is an AI-native financial operating system for health systems, founded by experienced entrepreneurs and backed by top-tier investors. We give finance, supply chain, and managed care teams real-time visibility into where margin is at risk, with a precise action plan for capturing it before it's gone.

Purpose-built for healthcare's complexity, Midstream transforms structured, unstructured, and external data into instant, contract-aware insights that drive smarter decisions. Our AI agents unify spend and revenue operations across the full back-office, learning and adapting with every interaction and delivering always-on intelligence that no point solution or one-off engagement can match.

We're compressing months to minutes and turning post-period hindsight into in-the-moment foresight. The tempo of healthcare finance is changing, and Midstream is leading it.


OPPORTUNITY

We are looking for a Product Marketing Manager to define how Midstream’s product and technology are understood in the market.

This role sits at the intersection of Product, Sales, and Marketing, translating Midstream’s platform and AI-enabled capabilities into clear narratives for healthcare financial and operational leaders. You will shape Midstream’s Ideal Customer Profile (ICP), product positioning, and messaging, ensuring both internal teams and external audiences understand how Midstream improves financial operations and reduces administrative complexity.

You will also help the market understand how agentic AI systems operate within healthcare workflows, translating complex technology into clear operational and financial outcomes.




WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING

Define the Ideal Customer Profile (ICP)

Partner with Product, Sales, and leadership to define Midstream’s ICP, buyer personas, and key financial and operational pain points.

Own product positioning and messaging

Develop the core product narrative, value proposition, and differentiation strategy.

Build sales enablement

Create the materials that support enterprise sales conversations, including product one-pagers, solution briefs, messaging frameworks, and competitive insights.

Translate product capabilities into clear storytelling

Develop narratives and visuals that explain how Midstream’s platform and AI agents operate within financial and operational workflows.

Support product launches

Partner with Product and Marketing to introduce new capabilities with clear messaging, sales readiness, and customer-facing materials.




WHO YOU ARE

A product storyteller

You translate complex technology into clear narratives that resonate with enterprise buyers.

Experienced in B2B product marketing

You’ve built positioning, messaging, and sales enablement for software products in partnership with Product and Sales.

Comfortable with technical products

You can clearly explain platforms involving AI, automation, or complex workflows.

A hands-on builder

You’re comfortable producing high-quality materials while helping shape the broader go-to-market narrative.

source_jobs.title: Product Lead
source_jobs.description:
Midstream is an AI-native financial operating system for health systems, founded by experienced entrepreneurs and backed by top-tier investors. We give finance, supply chain, and managed care teams real-time visibility into where margin is at risk, with a precise action plan for capturing it before it's gone.

Purpose-built for healthcare's complexity, Midstream transforms structured, unstructured, and external data into instant, contract-aware insights that drive smarter decisions. Our AI agents unify spend and revenue operations across the full back-office, learning and adapting with every interaction and delivering always-on intelligence that no point solution or one-off engagement can match.

We're compressing months to minutes and turning post-period hindsight into in-the-moment foresight. The tempo of healthcare finance is changing, and Midstream is leading it.




OPPORTUNITY

We are looking for a Product Lead to build and scale product experiences at Midstream using the latest frontier model capabilities.

This role sits at the intersection of customer, engineering, and product, turning real-world needs into shipped product. You will report directly to the VP of Product and own high-impact areas of the roadmap, helping translate strong market demand into focused, high-quality product execution.

Midstream has established product-market fit. The focus now is on expanding the product, deepening value, and delivering new capabilities quickly. You’ll work closely with customers and internal teams to identify what matters most, build MVPs fast, and evolve them into production-ready features—especially in areas powered by emerging AI technologies.

This is a high-ownership, execution-focused role. You’ll be expected to travel to customers as needed to stay close to real workflows and ensure what we build reflects how decisions are actually made.




WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING

Define and prioritize product bets

Synthesize customer, executive, and internal input to identify the highest-impact opportunities and drive clear product direction.

Own product development end-to-end

Drive features from idea through requirements, build, and launch, with a sharp focus on speed, quality, and business impact.

Work directly with customers

Engage with users to understand workflows, validate ideas, and ensure the product reflects real-world usage.

Partner closely with engineering and design

Translate product requirements into technically sound plans, balancing speed with long-term scalability.

Build and iterate quickly

Continuously identify new ways to build products on top of the latest model capabilities. Develop the product playbook, not follow a checklist.




WHO YOU ARE

A product builder with strong ownership

You’ve led product initiatives end-to-end and are comfortable owning outcomes. You know how to weight the risks and benefits of building with new model capabilities.

Customer-focused and execution-driven

You move quickly from problem → product and prioritize what drives real impact.

Technically fluent

You’re comfortable building with frontier models, discussing new data models, and working alongside system constraints. You can work closely with engineering through all these workflows.

Strong communicator

You bring clarity and urgency to complex problems and align stakeholders effectively.

Comfortable in dynamic environments

You’re willing to travel to customers and operate close to real-world workflows. You are constantly engaging with emerging research and industry trends to deliver innovative product experiences

source_jobs.title: Partnership Engagement
source_jobs.description:
Midstream is an AI-native financial operating system for health systems, founded by experienced entrepreneurs and backed by top-tier investors. We give finance, supply chain, and managed care teams real-time visibility into where margin is at risk, with a precise action plan for capturing it before it's gone.

Purpose-built for healthcare's complexity, Midstream transforms structured, unstructured, and external data into instant, contract-aware insights that drive smarter decisions. Our AI agents unify spend and revenue operations across the full back-office, learning and adapting with every interaction and delivering always-on intelligence that no point solution or one-off engagement can match.

We're compressing months to minutes and turning post-period hindsight into in-the-moment foresight. The tempo of healthcare finance is changing, and Midstream is leading it.




OPPORTUNITY

We are looking for a Partner Engagement Manager to own the success and outcomes of Midstream’s health system customers.

This role is responsible for ensuring customers realize value quickly and consistently. You will serve as the primary point of contact from day one—understanding customer goals, guiding adoption, and ensuring Midstream delivers measurable impact on financial and operational performance.

This is a high-ownership customer centric role. You will work closely with customers to turn initial adoption into long-term partnership—ensuring they see continuous value, expand their use of the platform over time, and become strong advocates for Midstream.




WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING

Own customer outcomes
Partner with customers to define success, track progress, and ensure Midstream delivers clear, measurable value.

Drive adoption and engagement
Guide onboarding, usage, and ongoing engagement to ensure the product becomes embedded in customer workflows.

Proactively manage account health
Monitor signals, identify risks early, and resolve issues before they impact retention or satisfaction.

Build deep, trusted relationships
Develop strong relationships across stakeholders—from operators to executives—becoming a trusted partner over time.

Turn customers into advocates
Create positive customer experiences that lead to references, case studies, and executive sponsorship.

Support expansion through value
Identify opportunities to deepen engagement as customers realize success, partnering with the commercial team where needed.




WHO YOU ARE

Experienced in customer success or customer-facing roles
You have 5+ years of experience working closely with customers in SaaS, healthcare, or enterprise environments.

Outcome-oriented
You focus on delivering measurable value and ensuring customers achieve their goals—not just managing relationships.

A strong relationship builder
You build trust across all levels of an organization, including executive stakeholders.

Proactive and solution-oriented
You anticipate challenges, ask the right questions, and bring solutions before issues escalate.

Commercially aware (but not sales-first)
You understand how value leads to expansion and can spot opportunities without forcing them.

A high-ownership operator
You take responsibility for customer success, move quickly, and thrive in a fast-paced startup environment.

notes: AI-native financial operating system for health systems with multiple software engineer roles
